title: eriogonum microtheca var. spathulare
intro: Eriogonum microtheca var. spathulare, also known as Sulfur Flower Buckwheat or Desert Buckwheat, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and other minor health issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum microtheca var. spathulare include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and combat infections, making it a valuable herb for treating various skin and minor health conditions. The plant can be prepared as a salve or infused oil to be used topically, or made into a tea or tincture for internal use, showcasing its versatility as a medicinal herb.
